paper_title: The Surface-Topography Challenge: A Multi-Laboratory Benchmark Study to Advance the Characterization of Topography
source_file: 机理文献/条件备选/03_The surface-topography challenge A multi-laboratory benchmark study to advance the characterization.pdf
authors_note: A. Pradhan et al.; 153 authors in total. The full author and affiliation list was checked on PDF p.24-26.

visual_review.layout_notes: The two-column reading order, figure panels and captions, equation numbers, Greek symbols, subscripts, integration limits, units and PDF page numbering were checked against rendered pages. The three retained crops were cut from 240 dpi renders and re-opened to confirm complete axes, legends, scale bars and captions.

sections_checked: Section 1 Introduction: The Purpose of this Challenge
Section 2 Methods for this Challenge: Creating Samples and Collecting Results
Section 3 Challenge Submissions and Sections 3.1-3.5 measurement techniques and file formats
Section 4 Data Analysis and Discussion, Sections 4.1-4.6
Section 5 Lessons Learned, Sections 5.1-5.3 and all three Topography Best Practices
Appendix C Results and Discussion on Scattering Techniques
Appendix D Statistical Parameters and Detrending
Appendix E Power Spectral Density, Sections E.1-E.4
Data Availability, references and author/affiliation pages checked for document completeness

equations_checked.notes: Continuous and discrete RMS height, the two-dimensional area expression, the quadratic reference line and quadratic trend plane were visually checked. The card retains D1-D2 and the detrending conditions; it warns that the paper aggregates area maps line by line.

Forward Fourier transform, inverse Fourier sum and continuum inverse transform were checked, including q_n = 2 pi n/L and Delta q = 2 pi/L. Only the forward convention needed by the retained PSD definition is displayed in the card.

The missing-region Laplace equation, boundary condition and minimum-gradient property were checked. The card keeps the boundary-value rule but does not interpret imputed values as recovered physical microtopography.
Mean-square-height preservation and normalized-window condition were checked. The paper uses a normalized Hann window for all reported PSDs.
The one-dimensional PSD definition C(q) = <|h-tilde(q)|^2>/L and h_rms-squared integral were checked, including the 1/pi factor and ensemble average over adjacent lines.

tables_checked.notes: The file-format list was checked for context but omitted because the current task needs processing provenance, not a complete inventory of vendor formats. X3P and open, documented formats are retained only as a qualitative interoperability recommendation in the route evidence.

figures_checked.reason: Sample fabrication and technique montages are replaceable by concise text and do not carry the retained quantitative quality-control logic.
The raw RMS-height spread and IQR binning procedure are preserved numerically and procedurally; Fig. 6 and Fig. 9 more compactly retain the scale and correction consequences.
Uniquely combines the as-submitted PSD spread, reported instrument resolution, visible artifacts and the resolution-corrected median/IQR spectra.
Directly contrasts agreement and disagreement between the same two measurement principles and cannot be replaced without losing the cross-technique quality-control demonstration.
Its three artifact mechanisms are retained in M2 and are also represented by the artifact insets and resolution cutoffs in the extracted Fig. 6.
Shows the final consensus RMS/PSD curves and the scale-dependent reversal from strong large-scale difference to small-scale similarity.
Submission counts and scattering intensity curves do not add a transferable microspine/topography mechanism; scattering inversion is retained as a warning.

omitted_sections: Detailed CrN deposition recipe in Appendix A
Complete proprietary and generic file-format inventory in Appendix B
Scattering-theory background and reference-only numerical examples in Appendix C
Acknowledgements, complete references and affiliation details

uncertain_items.item: Majority-rule consensus is not an external truth standard
uncertain_items.detail: The authors intentionally used a non-subjective majority rule, sometimes removing an entire technique for a surface. A minority technique could in principle be correct; this procedure is retained only as benchmark-specific robust aggregation.

For missing reports, 20 nm for AFM and 2 micrometers for stylus/indenter were assigned, while all optical techniques received a 2 micrometer cutoff. These are analysis defaults for this dataset, not universal instrument specifications.
The paper states that multiple PSDs can produce the same scattering profile, so ARS/XRR results were not combined into the consensus topography. No scattering-derived PSD was retained.

known_warnings: The benchmark surfaces are CrN-coated silicon, not porous fired-clay brick; none of the reported RMS or PSD values is a red-brick parameter.
The paper contains no microspine contact, finite-tip accessibility, friction, capture probability, load sharing or material-damage experiment.
Pixel spacing is not physical lateral resolution; optical transfer limits and probe radius must be recorded and applied before using measured small-scale topography.
The paper computes area-map PSDs line by line, so the retained one-dimensional convention must not be mistaken for a complete two-dimensional anisotropic spectrum.
Quadratic detrending removes long-wavelength content and produces a low-wavevector PSD downturn that the authors do not correct.
Harmonic imputation and Hann windowing are processing choices; large missing regions and different windows require sensitivity checks.
PSD numerical values depend on Fourier-transform normalization and units; imported spectra from other software require convention checks.
Median/IQR and majority rule are robust quality-control methods, not a substitute for instrument calibration or an absolute topography standard.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs in evidence_card.md; equations, Latin identifiers and Markdown syntax excluded. The card uses the permitted 3500-character ceiling because it retains independent measurement-QA, robust-consensus and spectral-processing interfaces.
